rationale: The citing paper uses Shiv, Carmon, and Ariely (2005) to support a core theoretical claim that consumer effects can operate outside conscious awareness. In its theory section, it explicitly draws on that paper's finding that pricing can alter efficacy without awareness, and in Study 1b it cites the paper again as part of the established approach for testing consciousness of effects. This is more than a passing background mention because it helps justify both the nondeliberate mechanism the authors propose and how they test awareness empirically.
relation_description: The citing paper applies Shiv et al.'s nonconscious marketing-effect insight to a different phenomenon: upgrade-driven carelessness toward owned products. It also borrows that precedent to motivate its awareness-testing approach in the empirical studies.
